title: Drive Health Deep Check
description: Inspect drive health with SMART/storage/event evidence before any repair.
starter_prompt: Check my drive health deeply. Use read-only checks first and explain any repair risk.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Physical disks =='; Get-PhysicalDisk | Select-Object FriendlyName,SerialNumber,MediaType,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,Size | Format-List; Write-Output '== Disk drives =='; Get-CimInstance Win32_DiskDrive | Select-Object Model,SerialNumber,MediaType,Status,Size,InterfaceType | Format-List; Write-Output '== Volumes =='; Get-Volume | Select-Object DriveLetter,FileSystemLabel,FileSystem,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,SizeRemaining,Size | Format-List; Write-Output '== Partitions =='; Get-Partition | Select-Object DiskNumber,PartitionNumber,DriveLetter,Type,Size | Format-List

phases.prompt: Collect read-only physical disk, drive, volume, and partition inventory.
phases.completion: Target drive inventory is captured or ambiguity is visible.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; function TrySection($n,[scriptblock]$b){ Section $n; try { & $b } catch { Write-Output ('[warning] ' + $n + ' unavailable: ' + $_.Exception.Message) } }; TrySection 'Storage reliability counters' { try { $counters=Get-PhysicalDisk -ErrorAction Stop | Get-StorageReliabilityCounter -ErrorAction Stop; if($counters){ $counters | Select-Object DeviceId,Temperature,ReadErrorsTotal,WriteErrorsTotal,ReadErrorsUncorrected,WriteErrorsUncorrected,Wear,PowerOnHours,PowerCycleCount | Format-List } else { Write-Output '[warning] No storage reliability counters returned.' } } catch { Write-Output ('[warning] Storage reliability counters unavailable without administrator/storage CIM access: ' + $_.Exception.Message); Write-Output 'Fallback checks below are still valid but less complete than storage reliability counters.' } }; TrySection 'SMART predictive status fallback' { Get-CimInstance -Namespace root\wmi -ClassName MSStorageDriver_FailurePredictStatus -ErrorAction Stop | Select-Object InstanceName,PredictFailure,Reason | Format-List }; TrySection 'Disk fallback status' { Get-PhysicalDisk | Select-Object FriendlyName,SerialNumber,MediaType,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,Size | Format-List; Get-CimInstance Win32_DiskDrive | Select-Object Model,SerialNumber,Status,Size,InterfaceType | Format-List; Get-Disk -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Number,FriendlyName,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,PartitionStyle,Size,IsBoot,IsSystem | Format-List }; TrySection 'Recent disk/storage event summary' { $events=Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{LogName='System'; StartTime=(Get-Date).AddDays(-30)} -MaxEvents 300 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.ProviderName -match '^(disk|Disk|Ntfs|storahci|stornvme)$' }; if($events){ $events | Group-Object ProviderName,Id,LevelDisplayName | Sort-Object Count -Descending | Select-Object Count,Name | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Recent disk/storage event details =='; $events | Select-Object -First 80 TimeCreated,ProviderName,Id,LevelDisplayName,Message | Format-List } else { Write-Output 'No disk/Ntfs/storahci/stornvme events found in the last 30 days.' } }; TrySection 'File system scan state' { $drives=Get-CimInstance Win32_LogicalDisk -Filter 'DriveType=3' | Select-Object -ExpandProperty DeviceID; foreach($drive in $drives){ Write-Output ('fsutil dirty query ' + $drive); fsutil dirty query $drive 2>&1 }; Write-Output '== Volume free space =='; Get-Volume | Select-Object DriveLetter,FileSystemLabel,HealthStatus,SizeRemaining,Size | Format-List }

phases.prompt: Collect read-only SMART/storage reliability counters when accessible, fallback disk health/status, disk/Ntfs/storage events, free space, and file-system scan indicators. Treat any '[warning]' lines as missing evidence that must be disclosed.
phases.completion: Hardware and file-system evidence is captured and missing admin-only evidence is explicit.

phases.prompt: Analyze collected drive inventory and health evidence. Separate hardware risk from file-system risk, call out missing storage reliability counters when permission-limited, and do not treat fallback-only evidence as a complete SMART pass.
phases.completion: Evidence covers hardware and file-system signals.

phases.prompt: Classify drive risk and decide whether repairs, backups, vendor diagnostics, or elevated re-run are appropriate.
If repair is needed, propose least-risk commands and rollback notes. Do not execute without overseer lease.
phases.completion: Plan separates read-only verification from repair.

phases.prompt: If any repair command is recommended, ask human or apply access-mode policy and offer restore point before execution.
phases.completion: Risk gate is resolved or repair remains recommendation-only.

phases.prompt: Check whether drive identity, hardware health, file-system evidence, and repair risk have enough support for the recommendation. Verify missing admin-only storage counters are disclosed if present.
phases.completion: Evidence gaps or confidence are clear.

phases.prompt: Create a technician-readable drive report with drive identity, evidence, actions/tools used, verification status, artifacts/logs, cleanup status, residual risk, and safe next actions. Include missing-evidence warnings when any command section reported '[warning]'.
Preserve diagnostic logs and reports; remove only temporary scratch files created by this workflow.

acceptance_criteria: No write repair command runs before approval.
Hardware health and file-system health are separated.
Warnings cite event IDs or command evidence when available.
Permission-denied or missing storage reliability counters are explicitly disclosed.
Backup advice is included when failure risk is non-trivial.
